cases.title: POST registration succeeds with quick response under normal single-user load
preconditions: Published event RegistrationOpen with available seats
Authenticated participant with no active registration
Normal load test environment (single user, not peak spike per R-01)
Compose Postgres db service healthy via npm run aih:dev:db:up
steps: Record start time immediately before POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations with Authorization and Idempotency-Key
Await HTTP response and record elapsed time
Verify response body contains state Registered and registrationId
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with Registered assignment; elapsed time meets NFR-04 quick response under normal load; testing-plan §3 row 'Register with available seat → Registered' satisfied without perceptible delay blocking participant flow

cases.title: registrationService.register completes promptly with correct state on Postgres
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with seats available
Participant has no active registration
DATABASE_URL points to Compose Postgres with migrations applied
Normal load (sequential single call, no burst)
steps: Measure elapsed time for registrationService.register(eventId, participantId, actorContext)
Inspect returned registration state and database row
cases.expected: Registration commits with state Registered; service call completes within acceptable latency for NFR-04 under normal load; transactional path on Postgres does not introduce unnecessary round-trips

cases.title: POST registrations p95 latency under normal load
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with moderate existing registration volume (well below capacity)
Pool of authenticated participants each without active registration for the event
Normal load profile: steady sequential or low-concurrency requests, not R-01 peak spike
steps: Execute at least 50 POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations requests from distinct participants
Collect per-request elapsed times from client to complete HTTP response
Compute p95 latency across successful registrations
cases.expected: p95 response time meets NFR-04 quick response under normal load; all successful responses return HTTP 200 with valid registration payload; no systematic slowdown as moderate volume accumulates

cases.title: GET registration-status p95 latency under normal load
preconditions: Event with moderate registration volume
Authenticated participants with existing Registered or Waitlisted registrations
Normal load environment suitable for near real-time status refresh per FR-10
steps: Execute at least 50 GET /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registration-status requests across participants with known registrations
Collect per-request elapsed times
Compute p95 latency
cases.expected: p95 response time meets NFR-04 quick response under normal load; status queries remain suitable for participant UI refresh without blocking interaction

cases.title: POST cancel registration responds within acceptable latency under normal load
preconditions: Registered participants eligible to cancel before CancellationDeadline
Normal load environment (single or low-concurrency cancellations)
Event policy permits participant self-cancel
steps: Execute POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations/{registrationId}/cancel for multiple eligible registrations
Measure elapsed time per request from client to complete HTTP response
Compute p95 latency across successful cancellations
cases.expected: p95 cancel response time meets NFR-04 quick response under normal load; HTTP 200 with CancelledByUser returned without undue delay; cancellation remains a responsive registration action

cases.title: Registration transactional path avoids N+1 queries that would slow normal-load responses
preconditions: Registration module wired to Postgres adapter per docker-compose local runtime policy
Event with existing registrations below capacity
Database query logging or profiler enabled for test run
steps: Invoke registrationService.register for a new participant
Inspect SQL/query count for the registration transaction (capacity check, insert, state transition)
Measure service-layer elapsed time for the operation
cases.expected: Registration completes in bounded query count without N+1 fan-out; service elapsed time consistent with NFR-04 quick response under normal load; row-lock and in-transaction seat count pattern does not add excessive latency

cases.title: Waitlisted registration POST responds within acceptable latency
preconditions: Event at capacity with waitlistEnabled true
Authenticated participant with no active registration
Normal load (single request, not concurrent burst)
steps: Measure elapsed time for POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations when event is full
Inspect response state and waitlistPosition
cases.expected: HTTP 200 with state Waitlisted returned within NFR-04 quick response threshold; testing-plan §3 row 'Register when full + waitlist enabled → Waitlisted' satisfied; waitlist path is not materially slower than Registered path under normal load

cases.title: Moderate concurrent registration burst maintains acceptable p95 without peak-spike degradation
preconditions: Event RegistrationOpen with capacity comfortably above concurrent attempt count
10–20 unique participants without prior registration
Normal-to-moderate load (below R-01 peak spike scenario); not stress-test overload
steps: Fire parallel POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations from all participants simultaneously
Collect elapsed times for all settled responses
Compute p95 latency and verify capacity invariant
cases.expected: p95 remains within NFR-04 quick response under normal load; Registered count never exceeds capacity; no request hangs beyond acceptable timeout; distinguishes normal concurrent load from R-01 overload mitigation

cases.title: Duplicate registration rejection responds quickly without lock-induced delay
preconditions: Participant with existing active Registered registration for the event
Normal load environment
steps: Measure elapsed time for POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations from participant with active registration
Repeat several times to capture p95 for rejection path
cases.expected: HTTP 409 (or equivalent conflict) returned within NFR-04 quick response threshold; testing-plan §3 row 'Duplicate registration attempt → blocked' satisfied on error path; duplicate guard does not block on long-held locks; error path remains responsive per NFR-13 immediate feedback expectation

cases.title: Registration at near-capacity event responds within acceptable latency
preconditions: Event with capacity N and exactly N-1 Registered participants
One remaining seat available
Normal load single registration attempt
steps: Measure elapsed time for registrationService.register claiming the last available seat
Verify Registered state and capacity invariant after commit
cases.expected: Last-seat registration completes within NFR-04 quick response under normal load; transactional row lock and in-transaction seat count do not cause unacceptable latency for the final seat assignment

cases.title: Registration rejection when full without waitlist responds quickly
preconditions: Event at capacity with waitlistEnabled false
Authenticated participant with no active registration
Normal load environment
steps: Measure elapsed time for POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations
Inspect error response code and message
cases.expected: Rejection returned within NFR-04 quick response threshold; participant receives prompt failure feedback without waiting through slow capacity scans

cases.title: Participant registration UI provides immediate feedback under normal campus-network conditions
preconditions: Web and API running locally or in dev preview stack
Event detail page with registration open and available seats
Participant logged in on typical campus-network latency profile (not throttled to failure)
steps: Navigate to event detail and click register (or equivalent primary action)
Observe time until success or failure feedback appears (badge, toast, or status change)
Verify no prolonged spinner without resolution beyond acceptable interactive threshold
cases.expected: UI reflects registration outcome with immediate feedback per NFR-13; end-to-end registration action perceived as quick under normal load aligning with NFR-04; status updates to Registered without requiring manual page reload

cases.title: Registration performance assertions execute against Compose Postgres not in-memory stores
preconditions: npm run aih:dev:db:up has started healthy db service
DATABASE_URL=postgresql://we_event:we_event@localhost:5432/we_event
Forbidden persistence modes (in-memory Map, SQLite, mock-only repos) are not used per docker-compose local runtime policy
steps: Run integration or e2e latency scenarios for registration actions against Postgres
Verify measurements reflect real database I/O and transactional behavior
cases.expected: NFR-04 latency measurements are meaningful against durable Postgres storage; performance regressions from in-memory shortcuts are prevented by harness runtime policy

cases.title: GET my registrations responds within acceptable latency for registration status browsing
preconditions: Authenticated participant with multiple registrations across events
Normal load environment
Paginated request page=1 pageSize=20
steps: Measure elapsed time for GET /api/v1/me/registrations?page=1&pageSize=20
Repeat at least 30 times or across representative participants
Compute p95 latency
cases.expected: p95 response time meets NFR-04 quick response under normal load; single API call returns paginated slice without N+1 fan-out per testing plan AC-14 pattern; suitable for participant registration list views

cases.title: Unauthenticated POST registration returns 401 quickly without slow auth probe
preconditions: Published event RegistrationOpen with available seats
No Authorization header or expired/invalid token
Normal load environment (single request)
steps: Record start time immediately before POST /api/v1/events/{eventId}/registrations without Authorization
Await HTTP response and record elapsed time
Verify no registration row is created for the attempt
cases.expected: HTTP 401 Unauthorized returned within NFR-04 quick response threshold; AC-15 unauthenticated access denial satisfied; auth rejection path does not block on expensive downstream work; no registration side effects
